articles.title: IRGC says missile attack on Israel was a ‘warning’
articles.summary: IRGC says missile attack on Israel was a ‘warning’ Iran’s Revolutionary Guards say their missile attack on Israel on Sunday night was a “warning” after Israel struck Beirut earlier in the day. The IRGC threatened a wider response if Israel carries out further attacks, raising the risk of a broader regional confrontation. “Tonight's operation was a warning. If such aggressions are repeated, the responses will be broader and will cover all US-Zionist targets in the region,” the force said.

articles.title: IRGC says it targeted Israel’s Ramat David airbase
articles.summary: IRGC says it targeted Israel’s Ramat David airbase Iran’s Revolutionary Guards say they targeted Israel’s Ramat David airbase with ballistic missiles, as the regional confrontation fuelled by Israel’s attacks on Lebanon and Iran widens. The IRGC described the strike as a warning and cautioned that any further escalation would trigger a broader response.

articles.title: Iran says frozen assets are not US war spoils
articles.summary: Iran says frozen assets are not US war spoils Iran has rejected reports that the United States could use frozen Iranian assets to compensate Gulf allies for war-related damage. Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi said regional governments were “not in a position to demand reparations”, after Reuters reported that Washington could make Iranian assets available to Gulf allies for rebuilding and repairs tied to future damage caused by Iran. In a post on X, Gharibabadi said Iran’s assets were “neither war spoils for Washington nor a payment fund for its allies”.

articles.title: Israeli drone strike kills four Palestinians in Gaza City
articles.summary: Israeli drone strike kills four Palestinians in Gaza City An Israeli drone strike has killed four Palestinians, including a woman, northwest of Gaza City, raising the day’s death toll from Israeli attacks to 13. Medical sources at Al-Shifa Hospital said the Israeli drone fired a missile at a civilian vehicle and passersby near Al-Buraq School in the al-Nasr neighbourhood. Several people were wounded in the attack, some critically, as Israel continued to strike civilians across the besieged enclave. More than 30 people have been wounded in Israeli attacks across Gaza since the start of the day.

articles.title: Israeli strikes kill at least nine in Gaza
articles.summary: Israeli strikes kill at least nine in Gaza Israeli strikes on different locations in Gaza killed at least nine people and wounded 20 others, Palestinian health officials said on Sunday. One strike hit next to a large tent encampment of displaced families in Khan Younis in the south of the enclave, killing five and wounding 16 others. Later on Sunday, a separate Israeli air strike killed four people and wounded four others when it hit a vehicle driving through the middle of Gaza City. articles.title: Trump calls for more 'surgical' strikes on Hezbollah
articles.summary: Trump calls for more 'surgical' strikes on Hezbollah US President Donald Trump has called for more "surgical" attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon, while at the same time saying he was "very close" to a deal with Iran. The comments, made to NBC's Meet the Press programme in an interview recorded on Friday but broadcast on Sunday, have been interpreted as a tacit blessing of Israeli strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs. “I would like to see Lebanon experience a better life. I would like to see a more surgical attack against Hezbollah. I think it should be more surgical,” Trump said.

articles.title: Can a Forgotten Caribbean Town Become a Spaceport?
articles.summary: The Dominican Republic’s plan to build a commercial spaceport in the small town of Oviedo is about far more than rockets. Launch on Demand founder Burton Catledge says the project could create thousands of jobs, attract new investment, and help diversify the country beyond tourism. Supporters envision a new hub for aerospace, technology, and education, while local residents hope the project can bring opportunities to a region that has long felt overlooked. But before launches begin, developers must build the infrastructure, workforce, and regulatory framework needed to support an entirely new
